Name: APawn
Description:
Pawn is the base class of all actors that can be possessed by players or AI.
  They are the physical representations of players and creatures in a level.

Variables.Name: bUseControllerRotationPitch
Variables.Description: If true, this Pawn's pitch will be updated to match the Controller's ControlRotation pitch, if controlled by a PlayerController.

Variables.Name: bUseControllerRotationYaw
Variables.Description: If true, this Pawn's yaw will be updated to match the Controller's ControlRotation yaw, if controlled by a PlayerController.

Variables.Name: bUseControllerRotationRoll
Variables.Description: If true, this Pawn's roll will be updated to match the Controller's ControlRotation roll, if controlled by a PlayerController.

Variables.Name: bCanAffectNavigationGeneration
Variables.Description:
If set to false (default) given pawn instance will never affect navigation generation. 
	 	Setting it to true will result in using regular AActor's navigation relevancy 
	 	calculation to check if this pawn instance should affect navigation generation
	 	Use SetCanAffectNavigationGeneration to change this value at runtime.
	 	Note that modifying this value at runtime will result in any navigation change only if runtime navigation generation is enabled.

Variables.Name: BaseEyeHeight
Variables.Description: Base eye height above collision center.

Variables.Name: AutoPossessPlayer
Variables.Description:
Determines which PlayerController, if any, should automatically possess the pawn when the level starts or when the pawn is spawned.
	  @see AutoPossessAI

Variables.Name: AutoPossessAI
AIControllerClass
Variables.Description: Default class to use when pawn is controlled by AI.

Variables.Name: PlayerState
Variables.Description: If Pawn is possessed by a player, points to his playerstate.  Needed for network play as controllers are not replicated to clients.

Variables.Name: RemoteViewPitch
Variables.Description: Replicated so we can see where remote clients are looking.

Variables.Name: LastHitBy
Variables.Description: Controller of the last Actor that caused us damage.

Variables.Name: ControlInputVector
Variables.Description:
Accumulated control input vector, stored in world space. This is the pending input, which is cleared (zeroed) once consumed.
	  @see GetPendingMovementInputVector(), AddMovementInput()

Variables.Name: LastControlInputVector
Variables.Description:
The last control input vector that was processed by ConsumeMovementInputVector().
	  @see GetLastMovementInputVector()

Functions.Name: GetMovementComponent
Functions.Description: Return our PawnMovementComponent, if we have one. By default, returns the first PawnMovementComponent found. Native classes that create their own movement component should override this method for more efficiency.

Functions.Name: GetMeshComponent
Functions.Description: Return our Mesh, if we have one. By default, returns the first MeshComponent found. Native classes that create their own mesh component should override this method for more efficiency.

Functions.Name: PawnMakeNoise
Functions.Description:
Inform AIControllers that you've made a noise they might hear (they are sent a HearNoise message if they have bHearNoises==true)
	  The instigator of this sound is the pawn which is used to call MakeNoise.

Params.Name: Loudness
Params.Description: - is the relative loudness of this noise (range 0.0 to 1.0). Directly affects the hearing range specified by the AI's HearingThreshold.

Params.Name: NoiseLocation
Params.Description: - Position of noise source. If zero vector, use the actor's location.

Params.Name: bUseNoiseMakerLocation
Params.Description: - If true, use the location of the NoiseMaker rather than NoiseLocation. If false, use NoiseLocation.

Params.Name: NoiseMaker
Params.Description: - Which actor is the source of the noise. Not to be confused with the Noise Instigator, which is responsible for the noise (and is the pawn on which this function is called). If not specified, the pawn instigating the noise will be used as the NoiseMaker

Functions.Name: GetMovementBaseActor
Functions.Description: Gets the owning actor of the Movement Base Component on which the pawn is standing.

Functions.Name: IsControlled
Functions.Description: See if this actor is currently being controlled

Functions.Name: GetController
Functions.Description: Returns controller for this actor.

Functions.Name: GetControlRotation
Functions.Description: Get the rotation of the Controller, often the 'view' rotation of this Pawn.

Functions.Name: OnRep_Controller
Functions.Description: Called when Controller is replicated

Functions.Name: OnRep_PlayerState
Functions.Description: PlayerState Replication Notification Callback

Functions.Name: SetCanAffectNavigationGeneration
Functions.Description:
Use SetCanAffectNavigationGeneration to change this value at runtime.
	 	Note that calling this function at runtime will result in any navigation change only if runtime navigation generation is enabled.

Functions.Name: GetNavAgentLocation
Functions.Description: Basically retrieved pawn's location on navmesh

Functions.Name: ReceivePossessed
Functions.Description: Event called when the Pawn is possessed by a Controller (normally only occurs on the serverstandalone).

Functions.Name: ReceiveUnpossessed
Functions.Description: Event called when the Pawn is no longer possessed by a Controller.

Return.Description: true if controlled by a local (not network) Controller.
true if controlled by a human player (possessed by a PlayerController).

Functions.Name: GetBaseAimRotation
Functions.Description:
Return the aim rotation for the Pawn.
	  If we have a controller, by default we aim at the player's 'eyes' direction
	  that is by default the Pawn rotation for AI, and camera (crosshair) rotation for human players.

Functions.Name: DetachFromControllerPendingDestroy
Functions.Description: Call this function to detach safely pawn from its controller, knowing that we will be destroyed soon.

Functions.Name: SpawnDefaultController
Functions.Description: Spawn default controller for this Pawn, and get possessed by it.

Functions.Name: AddMovementInput
Functions.Description:
Add movement input along the given world direction vector (usually normalized) scaled by 'ScaleValue'. If ScaleValue < 0, movement will be in the opposite direction.
	  Base Pawn classes won't automatically apply movement, it's up to the user to do so in a Tick event. Subclasses such as Character and DefaultPawn automatically handle this input and move.

Params.Name: WorldDirection
Params.Description: Direction in world space to apply input

Params.Name: ScaleValue
Params.Description: Scale to apply to input. This can be used for analog input, ie a value of 0.5 applies half the normal value, while -1.0 would reverse the direction.

Params.Name: bForce
Params.Description: If true always add the input, ignoring the result of IsMoveInputIgnored().

Functions.Name: GetPendingMovementInputVector
Functions.Description:
Return the pending input vector in world space. This is the most up-to-date value of the input vector, pending ConsumeMovementInputVector() which clears it,
	  Usually only a PawnMovementComponent will want to read this value, or the Pawn itself if it is responsible for movement.

Return.Description: The pending input vector in world space.

Functions.Name: GetLastMovementInputVector
Functions.Description:
Return the last input vector in world space that was processed by ConsumeMovementInputVector(), which is usually done by the Pawn or PawnMovementComponent.
	  Any user that needs to know about the input that last affected movement should use this function.
	  For example an animation update would want to use this, since by default the order of updates in a frame is:
	  PlayerController (device input) -> MovementComponent -> Pawn -> Mesh (animations)

Return.Description: The last input vector in world space that was processed by ConsumeMovementInputVector().

Functions.Name: ConsumeMovementInputVector
Functions.Description:
Returns the pending input vector and resets it to zero.
	  This should be used during a movement update (by the Pawn or PawnMovementComponent) to prevent accumulation of control input between frames.
	  Copies the pending input vector to the saved input vector (GetLastMovementInputVector()).

Functions.Name: AddControllerPitchInput
Functions.Description:
Add input (affecting Pitch) to the Controller's ControlRotation, if it is a local PlayerController.
	  This value is multiplied by the PlayerController's InputPitchScale value.

Params.Name: Val
Params.Description: Amount to add to Pitch. This value is multiplied by the PlayerController's InputPitchScale value.

Functions.Name: AddControllerYawInput
Functions.Description:
Add input (affecting Yaw) to the Controller's ControlRotation, if it is a local PlayerController.
	  This value is multiplied by the PlayerController's InputYawScale value.

Params.Name: Val
Params.Description: Amount to add to Yaw. This value is multiplied by the PlayerController's InputYawScale value.

Functions.Name: AddControllerRollInput
Functions.Description:
Add input (affecting Roll) to the Controller's ControlRotation, if it is a local PlayerController.
	  This value is multiplied by the PlayerController's InputRollScale value.

Params.Name: Val
Params.Description: Amount to add to Roll. This value is multiplied by the PlayerController's InputRollScale value.

Functions.Name: IsMoveInputIgnored
Functions.Description: Helper to see if move input is ignored. If our controller is a PlayerController, checks Controller->IsMoveInputIgnored().

Functions.Name: LaunchPawn
Functions.Description: (Deprecated) Launch Character with LaunchVelocity

Functions.Name: K2_GetMovementInputVector
Functions.Description: (Deprecated) Return the input vector in world space.
